rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this ParameterChange (expired), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: source/anchor context was incomplete or not verifiably pinned. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: ParameterChange advances a credible public benefit worth its risk if the evidence holds. What would enable a directional vote: Provide No structured claim/evidence extraction from the proposal document; Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible). Residual uncertainty: Anchor document is not yet pinned locally for this action. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 47.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.020 (clamped to ±0.05): Doctrine-aware offline lean for a parameter action: parameter doctrine demands elevated scrutiny. Net bounded adjustment -0.020 (clamped to ±0.05).
top_fixes: Provide No structured claim/evidence extraction from the proposal document.
Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible).
Include SPO/operator impact envelope (CPU/RAM/network/storage) with measurable assumptions.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: no
Proposal claims not extracted (no cached anchor document text to read).
missing: No structured claim/evidence extraction from the proposal document.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: ParameterChange advances a credible public benefit worth its risk if the evidence holds.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of ParameterChange.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
